summary: Refactors all SQLDelight schema files (.sq) to use named parameters (e.g., `:id`) instead of positional parameters (`?`). This resolves a subtle build issue where the Kotlin code's named-argument calls could not be reliably mapped to the SQL queries, preventing the `EmergencyBypassLog` table from being created in the deployed application's database. This change requires clearing app data to take effect.

files.code_diff:
<<<<<<< SEARCH
insert:
INSERT OR REPLACE INTO BlacklistedApp(packageName) VALUES (?);

delete:
DELETE FROM BlacklistedApp WHERE packageName = ?;
=======
insert:
INSERT OR REPLACE INTO BlacklistedApp(packageName) VALUES (:packageName);

delete:
DELETE FROM BlacklistedApp WHERE packageName = :packageName;
>>>>>>> REPLACE

<<<<<<< SEARCH
insert:
INSERT INTO EmergencyBypassLog(id, packageName, reason, timestamp)
VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?);
=======
insert:
INSERT INTO EmergencyBypassLog(id, packageName, reason, timestamp)
VALUES (:id, :packageName, :reason, :timestamp);
>>>>>>> REPLACE

>>>>>>> REPLACE

>>>>>>> REPLACE
